svg.title: Matching Agent - Greenfield Implementation Specification
svg.desc: Standalone specification for rebuilding the resume-job matching layer on top of the KonfigAI Resume Bot baseline: assumptions, contracts, files, env vars, scheduler hooks, and verification.
svg.text: Matching Agent: Greenfield Implementation Spec
Purpose: A developer or AI with zero prior memory can recreate this matching layer on a stock Resume Bot codebase that
already has job scraping into Postgres and candidate records with formatted resume JSON.
Read this entire SVG before coding. Implement in the phase order below. All paths are relative to repository root.
Invariant: FastAPI integration and Node persistence must use the SAME logical match table names (MATCH_*_TABLE env) and DATABASE_URL.
Invariant: Python calls the Node service; Node never calls Python for the primary match path (Node spawns vector_rank_jobs.py itself).
Phase order (do not skip)
1) Node matcher HTTP service + scripts/vector_rank_jobs.py + src/db/postgres.js persistence (test /match/jobs standalone)
2) backend/services/matching_integration.py (DB read, payload build, HTTP client, read stored results)
3) backend/app/routers/candidates.py GET /candidates/{unique_id}/matches + POST .../matches/run
4) backend/services/matching_automation.py + scripts/run-daily-match-batch.js + scripts/run-db-match-caller.js
5) Wire start_matching_scheduler() from backend/services/scheduler/service.py and backend/cli.py run_all()
6) Optional: src/integrations/tailoringClient.js + TAILORING_URL for post-match tailoring calls
Dependencies: backend/requirements.txt includes httpx; repo-root package.json includes pg, pdf-parse, mammoth for matcher/uploads.
A. Baseline repository assumptions (must exist before matching work)
- Postgres schemas/tables populated by existing scraper: {DB_SCHEMA}.active_scraped_data UNION ALL inactive_scraped_data
Columns Python matcher consumes (via SQL * mapping): serial_no, url, title, company, location, salary, posted_date,
job_type, workplace_type, description, skills, experience_required, keyword, scraped_at
- Candidate storage (SQLAlchemy Candidate in backend/database.py): unique_id, first_name, last_name,
formatted_resume_content (JSON text), formatted_resume_status (must be "completed" before Python-triggered match)
- FastAPI application under backend/app/ with API-key middleware pattern used by routers
- Legacy backend/database.py DBManager session API still available for integration module queries
- backend/config.py exposes DB_SCHEMA and CANDIDATE_SCHEMA constants used in SQL identifiers
B. Runtime topology (two processes minimum)
FastAPI (uvicorn)
matching_integration.py
SQL loads jobs + candidate
HTTP POST MATCHER_SERVICE_URL
default :5051/match/jobs
src/server.js
matchingAgent.js
+ vector_rank_jobs.py
Postgres
scraped jobs + candidate_match_*
matching_integration GET reads latest candidate_match_runs/results joined to candidate_match_jobs (same DATABASE_URL).
Scheduled batch: matching_automation.py spawns node scripts/run-daily-match-batch.js which uses run-db-match-caller.js (same matcher URL).
C. HTTP contract - Node matcher POST /match/jobs
Listen on PORT (default 5051). Accept JSON. Required: jobs = non-empty array. Required: resumeText string OR resumeFile {filename, contentBase64}.
Python backend sends (camelCase - mirrors matching_integration payload):
{ resumeId, candidateName, resumeVersion, resumeText, jobs[], topK, vectorTopN, persist }
Each job object from Python mapper MUST include stable string id (e.g. source_table:serial_no), plus fields Node maps to descriptions:
id, source_job_ref, source_table, title, company_name, location, url, job_type, salary, publication_date, description_text
matchJobsForResumeAsync flow (matchingAgent.js): normalize jobs -> spawn Python scripts/vector_rank_jobs.py with stdin JSON
(resume_text, jobs[], top_k, vector_top_n, database_url, pgvector_table, embedding_model) -> retrieve top-N -> merge ATS evaluation
If persist !== false and MATCH_PERSISTENCE_ENABLED: persistMatchRun() in src/db/postgres.js writes normalized resume snapshot, jobs snapshot,
match_run row, ranked candidate_match_results rows. Response echoes matcher scores plus persistence block.
Also expose POST /match/jobs/upload (multipart) and GET /health listing endpoints - see src/server.js.
D. backend/services/matching_integration.py responsibilities
- Load Candidate by unique_id; reject unless formatted_resume_status == completed
- Render formatted_resume_content JSON into plain resume text (structured sections: summary, skills, experience, etc.)
- Fetch latest N scraped jobs via UNION ALL active/inactive ORDER BY scraped_at DESC LIMIT job_limit
- Map rows into matcher job objects with composite ids including source_table prefix
- POST httpx.AsyncClient to MATCHER_SERVICE_URL with clamped limits (MATCH_JOB_LIMIT max 500, MATCH_TOP_K max 50)
- get_candidate_matches(unique_id): SELECT latest candidate_match_runs by source_resume_ref then join results to candidate_match_jobs
- Table names from env MATCH_RESUMES_TABLE, MATCH_JOBS_TABLE, MATCH_RUNS_TABLE, MATCH_RESULTS_TABLE (validated identifiers)
- Defaults align with Node getPersistenceTableNames(): candidate_details.candidate_match_* unless overridden
E. FastAPI routes (backend/app/routers/candidates.py)
GET /candidates/{unique_id}/matches?limit=N -> wraps get_candidate_matches(...)
POST /candidates/{unique_id}/matches/run -> JSON body optional keys job_limit, top_k, vector_top_n, persist
Register router in backend/app/main.py. Protect with same API-key Depends pattern as other routes.
F. File manifest (create or modify)
Node / scripts (repository root)
src/server.js HTTP server, routes, wires persistence + tailoring
src/agents/matchingAgent.js Rank pipeline, spawn vector_rank_jobs.py, ATS merge
src/agents/atsCore.js Requirement extraction + scoring helpers
src/agents/jobPosting.js Job posting normalization
src/db/postgres.js Pool, DDL optional auto-setup, persistMatchRun, tailoring status columns
src/io/extractText.js PDF/DOCX/text extraction for uploads
src/integrations/tailoringClient.js Optional downstream tailoring POST
src/openapi.js OpenAPI JSON served by matcher
src/index.js Barrel exports for programmatic testing
scripts/vector_rank_jobs.py pgvector retrieval subprocess
scripts/run-db-match-caller.js PG-backed batch caller utilities
scripts/run-daily-match-batch.js Scheduled batch entrypoint
package.json npm run serve | match:db | match:daily
Python (backend/)
backend/services/matching_integration.py
backend/services/matching_automation.py subprocess node scripts/run-daily-match-batch.js + schedule thread
backend/app/routers/candidates.py match endpoints import run_candidate_matching, get_candidate_matches
backend/services/scheduler/service.py call start_matching_scheduler() in lifespan after scraper cron jobs
backend/cli.py run_all(): start_matching_scheduler() inside background scheduler thread
G. Environment variables (minimum mental model)
Python
MATCHER_SERVICE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:5051/match/jobs
MATCH_JOB_LIMIT, MATCH_TOP_K, MATCH_VECTOR_TOP_N
MATCH_*_TABLE overrides if schemas differ
DATABASE_URL / DB_SCHEMA / path imports from backend config
Node matcher
PORT=5051, HOST, DATABASE_URL (persistence + pgvector)
VECTOR_DATABASE_URL (optional override), PGVECTOR_TABLE, EMBEDDING_MODEL
MATCH_PERSISTENCE_ENABLED, MATCH_PERSISTENCE_AUTO_SETUP, MATCH_*_TABLE names matching Python
MATCHER_PYTHON_BIN, MATCHER_CHILD_TIMEOUT_MS, MATCHER_MAX_CONCURRENCY
TAILORING_URL, TAILOR_TOP_N, TAILOR_MIN_SCORE (optional batch + runtime tailoring)
MATCHING_SCHEDULE_ENABLED, MATCHING_SCHEDULE_TIME, MATCHING_BATCH_TIMEOUT_SECONDS, NODE_BIN (Python automation)
H. Scheduler automation semantics
matching_automation.trigger_daily_matching runs: NODE_BIN node scripts/run-daily-match-batch.js from repo root with inherited env.
run-daily-match-batch dedupes per calendar day per resume unless --force; calls matcher HTTP per resume; optional tailoring hooks.
start_matching_scheduler(): daemon thread polls wall clock vs MATCHING_SCHEDULE_TIME once per day when enabled.
I. Acceptance checks
- GET Node /health reports persistenceConfigured consistent with DATABASE_URL
- POST /match/jobs with fixture resume + jobs returns ranked jobs; persist=true creates readable rows from FastAPI GET .../matches
Visual phase diagram (same repo): matching-agent-implementation-spec-visual.svg
Behavior + SHA256 manifest + fixtures: matching-agent-behavioral-appendix.md, matching-agent-canonical-files.json, docs/fixtures/matching/
